name: New Zealand Rock Cakes
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Orange marmalade (or preserve in jelly section of a store)
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 425°F.
Sift flour and salt into a mixing bowl, blending in butter or margarine with clean hands until it resembles fine bread crumbs.
Add sugar, raisins, and marmalade; mix well with a spoon.
Add egg and blend into the flour mixture until stiff and rocky.
Form golf ball-sized chunks of dough and place on a cookie sheet about 1 inch apart.
Do not smooth out the dough pieces.
Bake in the oven for 15 minutes or until lightly browned.
Remove from the oven and turn each rock cake bottom side up to cool.
Serve as a snack with milk or tea.
Rock cakes are one of the oldest known New Zealand recipes, dating back to 1861. They were popular tea-time treats among the original English settlers. Serves 6-8.
